domains.name: Domain 2: Configuration Management and Infrastructure as Code
Domain 4: Monitoring, Logging, and Incident Response

questions.text: A team provisions workloads with a minimal CloudFormation template that only sets required properties; optional defaults were applied outside the stack and now drift from the template. They want stack updates to realign resources and a low-effort way to catch manual changes. Which approach meets the goal with the LEAST custom engineering?

options.text: Publish a Lambda function that calls DetectStackDrift on a schedule via EventBridge and re-executes the current template when drift is found
Rely on stack exports only; ignore drift until the next full stack delete
Encode every property (including former defaults) in the template and use an AWS Config managed rule with automatic remediation via the AWS-UpdateCloudFormationStack SSM runbook
Manually compare the console with the template each week

questions.explanation: Scheduled drift detection plus redeploying the known template is a small, focused automation pattern. Full template enumeration plus Config remediation is powerful but heavier to roll out.

questions.text: Workers on EC2 consume an SQS queue. During spikes the fleet scales on average CPU, but messages still backlog because CPU stays low while queue depth grows. Which scaling approach is MOST reliable?

options.text: Publish a custom CloudWatch metric (visible messages ÷ in-service instances) and scale the Auto Scaling group on that metric
Attach an ALB and scale on ALBRequestCountPerTarget even though traffic is queue-driven
Change the target tracking policy to NumberOfMessagesReceived on the queue
Lower the CPU target and shorten the warm-up period only

questions.explanation: Queue depth per instance directly reflects backlog pressure; CPU is a poor proxy for worker pools pulling from SQS.

questions.text: An organization with AWS Organizations must watch service quota utilization across all member accounts daily and email owners when usage crosses 80% of a quota. What should they implement?

options.text: Enable Service Quotas integration with Organizations, create per-service CloudWatch alarms at 80% utilization, and notify an SNS topic in ALARM state
Run Trusted Advisor quota checks weekly from a single Lambda in the management account
Use the same alarms but route ALARM transitions through EventBridge instead of SNS
Refresh Trusted Advisor checks daily and open support cases for WARN results

questions.explanation: Service Quotas exposes utilization metrics suitable for CloudWatch alarms; SNS is the standard notification path when alarms fire.

questions.text: CodeDeploy performs blue/green releases behind an ALB. Operators today read application logs on instances and manually roll back when errors spike. Which TWO steps automate rollback with minimal delay? (Select TWO.)

options.text: Configure the deployment group to fail and roll back when a linked CloudWatch alarm enters ALARM
Schedule a Lambda hourly to call ContinueDeployment
Subscribe CodeDeploy directly to an SNS topic (unsupported target)
Ship logs to CloudWatch Logs, metric-filter error lines, and alarm on the error rate

questions.explanation: CodeDeploy can watch deployment alarms; log metric filters catch application errors described in files rather than ALB HTTP codes alone.

questions.text: Elastic Beanstalk was launched via the EB CLI with an .ebextensions file that sets instance type to t3.medium, but new environments still launch t3.small. What most likely explains the behavior?

options.text: CLI/API parameters and recommended values take precedence over .ebextensions for settings such as instance type
Instance type cannot be set in .ebextensions
The IAM user lacks permission for t3.medium

questions.explanation: Elastic Beanstalk applies settings in order: direct environment/API changes, saved config, .ebextensions, then defaults. CLI launches can set recommended values that win over extensions.

questions.text: API Gateway fronts Lambda for a production API. A new Lambda version is ready; the team needs 10% of traffic on the new version for one week with no client-side URL changes. What is the MOST efficient approach?

options.text: Create a second API stage and split traffic with a simple Route 53 record
Enable a canary release on the existing production stage (10% to new version, 90% to current)
Stand up a second API and weighted Route 53 records to a new hostname
Switch the integration to Lambda proxy and route inside the function

questions.explanation: API Gateway canary deployments shift traffic on the same stage customers already call.

questions.text: JSON application logs on EC2 include PII. A vendor needs near-real-time access to troubleshoot errors but must not see PII and cannot receive SSH. What is the MOST cost-effective pattern?

options.text: Central syslog on EC2 with a nightly Batch job copying filtered logs to S3
Stream logs with the CloudWatch agent; subscription filter + Lambda writes non-PII events to OpenSearch always on
Agent to Kinesis Data Firehose with Lambda transformation to S3
Agent to CloudWatch Logs; subscription filter + Lambda writes filtered events to S3 for vendor access

questions.explanation: CloudWatch Logs subscription filters can strip sensitive fields; S3 access is inexpensive for episodic vendor review without always-on search clusters.

questions.text: Across an AWS Organization, any S3 bucket that becomes public (ACL or policy) must be auto-remediated in every Region and account, including buckets created later. Which design fits?

options.text: EventBridge global endpoints replicate S3 events to a security account
Organization CloudTrail bucket triggers EventBridge in one account for all API calls
Enable S3 EventBridge notifications on each bucket for ACL changes
Deploy an AWS Config rule (org-wide) with automated remediation via an SSM Automation runbook

questions.explanation: Managed Config rules detect public access; remediation runbooks such as AWS-DisableS3BucketPublicReadWrite can run automatically per account/Region.

questions.text: Dev and UAT pipelines deploy the same Git branch but UAT shows a defect absent in Dev. How do you ensure identical artifacts and environments?

options.text: Lambda validates artifacts; separate CodeBuild projects per pipeline
One build pipeline publishes artifacts to S3; separate deploy pipelines pull the same object
Shared CodeBuild with different buildspec overrides per environment
Lambda compares artifact SHA-256 in UAT pipeline; both deploy stages use the same CloudFormation template

questions.explanation: A single template provisions equivalent stacks; checksum validation proves both pipelines consumed the same build output.

questions.text: A SAM HTTP API must validate a proprietary token header via an existing on-premises auth service (not SAML/OIDC). Which API Gateway mechanism applies?

options.text: Lambda authorizer that calls the legacy service and returns an IAM policy

questions.explanation: Lambda authorizers implement custom token validation logic in one function.

questions.text: CodeDeploy must target on-premises servers with rotating credentials and tag-based deployment groups. What is the MOST secure registration pattern?

options.text: Long-lived IAM user access keys in a local file
IAM user keys plus instance ID registration lists
IAM role with STS AssumeRole, periodic credential refresh, agent install, tag-based groups
Same as (c) but register instances by ID list instead of tags

questions.explanation: Temporary credentials from AssumeRole avoid static keys; deployment groups target tags, not manual ID lists.

questions.text: New EC2 instances behind an ALB must finish registration with an external audit API before receiving traffic. Which Auto Scaling integration ensures that?

options.text: Lifecycle hook in Pending:Wait; custom script registers; complete with CONTINUE or ABANDON
User data script only; return non-zero on failure
EventBridge schedule every 5 minutes to register instances
EventBridge on launch invoking Lambda asynchronously without blocking scale-out

questions.explanation: Lifecycle hooks pause launch until your script confirms readiness.

questions.text: Legal documents in S3 may be tagged LegalHold=true. Objects older than 90 days without that tag must expire automatically; uploads from apps that cannot add tags must still be deletable on schedule. Which TWO actions? (Select TWO.)

options.text: Deny PutObject unless LegalHold tag is present on upload
Lifecycle expiration after 90 days with no tag filter
S3 event → Lambda adds LegalHold=false when the tag is missing
Bucket policy denying delete when LegalHold=true using ExistingObjectTag
Lifecycle rule: age > 90 days AND LegalHold=false

questions.explanation: Defaulting the tag enables lifecycle filters; expiration with tag=false skips legal holds.

questions.text: ECS tasks stop with reason Essential container in task exited. Email notification is required with minimal code. SNS topic already exists. Next step?

options.text: EventBridge rule on ECS task STOPPED with that stoppedReason → SNS target
Lambda polling DescribeTasks on a schedule
Set notifications=true on the task definition

questions.explanation: EventBridge captures ECS events natively; SNS delivers mail without custom polling.

questions.text: Deploy API Gateway + Lambda + DynamoDB + ElastiCache Redis for a serverless web API with the least operational overhead. Preferred packaging?

options.text: Single AWS SAM template for all resources
CloudFormation only with custom resources for every component
CloudFormation for Redis; SAM for the rest

questions.explanation: SAM extends CloudFormation for serverless patterns in one deployable template.

questions.text: Developers launch EC2 from custom AMIs, but some instances use unapproved images. Automatic discovery and termination is required. What should you deploy?

options.text: SCP denying ec2:RunInstances attached to an IAM group
AWS Config rule with remediation that terminates noncompliant instances

questions.explanation: Config rules evaluate running instances; remediation can stop noncompliant hosts automatically.

questions.text: An Auto Scaling fleet drifts because operators SSH in and change packages. The company wants identical configuration at launch and over time. Which process?

options.text: New golden AMI per patch; OldestLaunchConfiguration termination; double capacity then scale in; block console access; use Systems Manager for maintenance
Use AWS-provided AMI and yum update in user data only
NewestInstance termination policy with Config maintenance
OldestLaunchConfiguration policy but mutate instances via SSH

questions.explanation: Replacing instances from a fresh AMI and discouraging manual drift aligns with immutable infrastructure.

questions.text: A public REST API’s Lambda now requires a language field in the JSON body. One production alias must serve everyone; legacy clients omit language. How should the team roll out?

options.text: New API only; delete old Lambda immediately
Point existing API to new Lambda and delete old function day one
New API for new clients; old API unchanged
New API for new clients; add mapping template on legacy API integration injecting language=English

questions.explanation: Mapping templates let the existing stage add defaults while a new API serves explicit language values.

questions.text: Multi-AZ RDS MySQL needs DR in a second Region with RTO < 2 hours and RPO < 10 minutes, cost-sensitive. Stateless web tier is on EC2 behind an ALB. Recommended DR pattern?

options.text: Elastic Beanstalk second Region; swap CNAME on disaster
Maintain AMIs and a cross-Region RDS read replica; CloudFormation stack in DR; promote replica; update DNS
Active-active Aurora global DB with cloned web tier always running

questions.explanation: Read replicas meet tight RPO; AMIs + IaC enable faster RTO without full dual-site cost.

questions.text: Python services in CodePipeline need static analysis for defects before compile. Which service integrates with the Git repo for pull-request feedback?

options.text: Associate the repository with CodeGuru Reviewer
CodeGuru Reviewer only inside CodeBuild without repo association

questions.explanation: CodeGuru Reviewer analyzes PRs for code quality and security issues in Python and Java.

questions.text: Operators want every optional CloudFormation property defined in code and drift corrected by re-applying the full template, using managed governance rather than a custom Lambda scheduler. What should they add?

options.text: DetectStackDrift Lambda on a cron only
Explicit properties in the template plus AWS Config drift detection with AWS-UpdateCloudFormationStack remediation

questions.explanation: Config’s CloudFormation drift check plus the standard remediation runbook updates stacks to match templates.

questions.text: A stateless tier must fail over DNS to a warm secondary Region. Database is RDS Multi-AZ in the primary Region only today. What improves Regional resilience for the app tier first?

options.text: Copy launch templates/AMIs to the DR Region and automate stack creation with Route 53 failover
Increase Auto Scaling max size in one Region

questions.explanation: Pre-staged AMIs and IaC in the DR Region shorten recovery; Route 53 health checks shift traffic.

questions.text: During a blue/green CodeDeploy cutover, target 5xx rates from the load balancer are not rising, but application log errors are. Which signal should drive automated rollback?

options.text: Deployment group linked to a log-derived error metric alarm
Manual SNS email to operators only

questions.explanation: Application errors may appear only in logs; metric filters surface them to CodeDeploy alarms.

questions.text: Security needs daily detection of public S3 buckets across many accounts without writing custom drift Lambdas per account. Which managed capability applies?

options.text: Organization-wide AWS Config conformance pack with public access rules and remediation
CloudFront in front of all buckets

questions.explanation: Conformance packs roll Config rules and remediation to all accounts in the organization.

questions.text: A team must shift 10% of Lambda alias traffic for alias `live` without changing the API invoke URL. Which feature applies?

options.text: CodeDeploy linear traffic shifting on the Lambda alias
Weighted target groups on an ALB in front of Lambda

questions.explanation: CodeDeploy supports canary and linear deployments for Lambda aliases behind the same integration.

questions.text: Chaos testing should inject EC2 stop failures in a non-production environment with guardrails. Which AWS service is purpose-built?

options.text: AWS Fault Injection Simulator experiment templates

questions.explanation: FIS provides controlled fault injection experiments with safety controls.

questions.text: For cost and security, the team must orchestrate CI/CD with manual approvals. What is the recommended approach? orchestrate CI/CD with manual approvals.

options.text: AWS CodePipeline with manual approval actions

questions.text: Operations reports a need to run tests in isolated build environments. What is the MOST operationally efficient solution? run tests in isolated build environments.

options.text: AWS CodeBuild with privileged mode only when needed

questions.text: For cost and security, the team must implement GitOps for EKS. What is the recommended approach? implement GitOps for EKS.

options.text: Flux/Argo CD on EKS or CodePipeline to cluster

questions.explanation: GitOps reconciles cluster state from Git.

questions.text: A regulated workload requires the organization to sign and verify container images. Which solution is BEST? sign and verify container images.

options.text: Notation/Cosign with ECR and admission controls

questions.text: An audit found gaps: the organization must deploy Lambda with canary traffic shifting. Which option addresses this? deploy Lambda with canary traffic shifting.
A company needs to store pipeline artifacts immutably. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort? store pipeline artifacts immutably.

options.text: CodePipeline S3 artifact store with encryption

questions.explanation: Artifacts should be versioned and encrypted.

questions.text: The DevOps team is automating releases and must trigger pipelines from Git webhooks securely. Which design fits best? trigger pipelines from Git webhooks securely.
questions.explanation: Connections use OAuth to external Git.

questions.text: While troubleshooting latency, engineers determine they must run compliance tests in pipeline. Which action is appropriate? run compliance tests in pipeline.

options.text: CloudFormation Guard or cfn-nag in CodeBuild

questions.text: Which service should you use to orchestrate CI/CD with manual approvals?

options.text: AWS CodePipeline with manual approval actions

questions.text: After a design review, leadership requires the team to define reusable infrastructure modules. Which approach is MOST reliable? define reusable infrastructure modules.

options.text: AWS CDK constructs or CloudFormation modules

questions.text: A company needs to detect configuration drift on servers. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort? detect configuration drift on servers.

options.text: Systems Manager Compliance and AWS Config

questions.text: A company needs to manage Ansible/Chef at scale. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort? manage Ansible/Chef at scale.

options.text: AWS OpsWorks or SSM State Manager

questions.text: A regulated workload requires the organization to import existing resources into IaC. Which solution is BEST? import existing resources into IaC.
questions.explanation: Resource import brings legacy under management.

questions.text: While troubleshooting latency, engineers determine they must use stack sets for multi-account rollout. Which action is appropriate? use stack sets for multi-account rollout.
The DevOps team is automating releases and must parameterize environments safely. Which design fits best? parameterize environments safely.

options.text: SSM Parameter Store hierarchies per env
Prod params in dev account shared

questions.text: During peak traffic, the platform team must validate templates before deploy. What should they do FIRST? validate templates before deploy.
For cost and security, the team must manage SAM transforms for serverless. What is the recommended approach? manage SAM transforms for serverless.
Which service should you use to define reusable infrastructure modules?

options.text: AWS CDK constructs or CloudFormation modules

questions.text: A company needs to run chaos experiments safely. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort? run chaos experiments safely.
A company needs to define RTO/RPO in resilience hub. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort? define RTO/RPO in resilience hub.
questions.explanation: Resilience Hub assesses apps against policies.

questions.text: For cost and security, the team must multi-AZ RDS with automatic failover. What is the recommended approach? multi-AZ RDS with automatic failover.
After a design review, leadership requires the team to queue-based load leveling. Which approach is MOST reliable? queue-based load leveling.

options.text: SQS with Auto Scaling on queue depth

questions.text: A regulated workload requires the organization to regional failover for stateless tier. Which solution is BEST? regional failover for stateless tier.

options.text: Route 53 failover + AMIs/Launch templates in DR Region

questions.explanation: Stateless tiers are easier to failover.

questions.text: After a design review, leadership requires the team to use circuit breaker pattern in microservices. Which approach is MOST reliable? use circuit breaker pattern in microservices.

options.text: API Gateway throttling and client retries with jitter

questions.text: The DevOps team is automating releases and must deploy ECS services across AZs. Which design fits best? deploy ECS services across AZs.
Operations reports a need to immutable infrastructure for EC2. What is the MOST operationally efficient solution? immutable infrastructure for EC2.

options.text: Auto Scaling with golden AMI/Image Builder

questions.explanation: Immutable deploys replace instances instead of patching live.

questions.text: Which service should you use to run chaos experiments safely?
What is the MOST appropriate AWS solution to run chaos experiments safely?
An audit found gaps: the organization must correlate logs metrics and traces. Which option addresses this? correlate logs metrics and traces.
While troubleshooting latency, engineers determine they must create composite alarms to reduce noise. Which action is appropriate? create composite alarms to reduce noise.
questions.explanation: Composite alarms combine child alarms logically.

questions.text: An audit found gaps: the organization must export logs to SIEM. Which option addresses this? export logs to SIEM.
During peak traffic, the platform team must audit privileged actions. What should they do FIRST? audit privileged actions.

options.text: CloudTrail management events and CloudTrail Lake

questions.explanation: CloudTrail Lake enables SQL analytics on events.

questions.text: A hybrid environment must support a goal to monitor CI/CD pipeline failures without changing application code. What should you implement? monitor CI/CD pipeline failures.
After a design review, leadership requires the team to track deployment frequency DORA metrics. Which approach is MOST reliable? track deployment frequency DORA metrics.
Operations reports a need to use Synthetics for critical user journeys. What is the MOST operationally efficient solution? use Synthetics for critical user journeys.
The DevOps team is automating releases and must alert on Auto Scaling failures. Which design fits best? alert on Auto Scaling failures.
questions.explanation: Lifecycle hooks enable debugging launch issues.

questions.text: Which service should you use to correlate logs metrics and traces?
What is the MOST appropriate AWS solution to correlate logs metrics and traces?
A team must correlate logs metrics and traces. Which option meets the requirement?
Which service should you use to create composite alarms to reduce noise?
questions.explanation: Composite alarms combine child alarms logically.

questions.text: While troubleshooting latency, engineers determine they must route operational events to targets. Which action is appropriate? route operational events to targets.
questions.explanation: EventBridge reacts to AWS API and custom events.

questions.text: After a design review, leadership requires the team to manage incidents with runbooks. Which approach is MOST reliable? manage incidents with runbooks.
During peak traffic, the platform team must auto-remediate non-compliant resources. What should they do FIRST? auto-remediate non-compliant resources.

options.text: SSM Automation documents triggered by Config

questions.text: A company needs to isolate compromised EC2 quickly. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort? isolate compromised EC2 quickly.

options.text: Security group isolation runbook or NACL deny

questions.text: While troubleshooting latency, engineers determine they must aggregate security findings for triage. Which action is appropriate? aggregate security findings for triage.
For cost and security, the team must capture forensics disk/memory. What is the recommended approach? capture forensics disk/memory.

options.text: SSM Automation snapshot volumes with preservation

questions.text: For cost and security, the team must notify on-call from alarms. What is the recommended approach? notify on-call from alarms.

options.text: SNS to AWS Chatbot or PagerDuty integration

questions.text: A hybrid environment must support a goal to document post-incident reviews without changing application code. What should you implement? document post-incident reviews.

options.text: Runbooks and knowledge base in Incident Manager

questions.text: The DevOps team is automating releases and must validate recovery procedures regularly. Which design fits best? validate recovery procedures regularly.
After a design review, leadership requires the team to use cell-based blast isolation for EKS. Which approach is MOST reliable? use cell-based blast isolation for EKS.
A hybrid environment must support a goal to protect against regional failure without changing application code. What should you implement? protect against regional failure.
A regulated workload requires the organization to ensure idempotent deployments. Which solution is BEST? ensure idempotent deployments.
Operations reports a need to load test before peak events. What is the MOST operationally efficient solution? load test before peak events.

options.text: Distributed Load Testing on AWS or third-party in VPC

questions.text: After a design review, leadership requires the team to use SQS visibility timeout correctly. Which approach is MOST reliable? use SQS visibility timeout correctly.
A company needs to design EKS control plane resilience. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort? design EKS control plane resilience.

options.text: Multi-AZ worker nodes and pod topology spread

questions.text: A hybrid environment must support a goal to backup EKS etcd application state without changing application code. What should you implement? backup EKS etcd application state.
questions.explanation: Velero backs up Kubernetes resources and PVs.

questions.text: Which service should you use to validate recovery procedures regularly?
What is the MOST appropriate AWS solution to validate recovery procedures regularly?
Which TWO practices align with the AWS Well-Architected Framework? (Select TWO.)

options.text: Design for failure and automate recovery
Use single-AZ for all production data stores

questions.explanation: Reliability and operational excellence emphasize automation, testing, and learning from failure.

questions.text: Which TWO official AWS resources help you prepare for AWS Certified DevOps Engineer – Professional? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: Use official AWS exam guides, Skill Builder, and AWS documentation—not real exam content.

questions.text: Which are AWS services used for encryption or key management? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: KMS manages keys; Secrets Manager stores and rotates secrets.
